title: Ajahn Funn: Let Buddho know. Our mind is calm, joyful. Buddho is joyful. Buddho is radiant. Buddho is the knower. Whatever arises, it knows completely.…
content:
Let Buddho Know: When We Don't Label, the Mind Is Freed from Suffering

Let Buddho know. Our mind is calm, joyful. Buddho is joyful. Buddho is radiant. Buddho is the knower. Whatever arises, it knows completely. Let us know saṅkhārā as they are, know consciousness as it is. Saṅkhārā are impermanent, consciousness is also entirely impermanent. We should not cling to them as self or being. If we don't cling, our mind is freed from suffering. If we don't label, it's freed. Our mind is freed from suffering. If we don't say "suffering," is there suffering? Don't say it. Don't label. In truth, there is no suffering or difficulty. Look at this physical body: after death, they invite monks to perform the bang-sukun (funeral cloth ceremony). They say they are "pulling" for the dead person. Actually, they are pulling for us living to see. In the past, that dead person was just like us – worried about this and that, clinging to this and that. Now that they are dead, what has become of them? They don't cling to anything. They used to complain of pain and aches, slap at mosquitoes, suffer from this pain and that. After being cremated, they don't complain at all. They still have complete legs, arms, ears, eyes, nose. Look at us all. This is why we practice kammaṭṭhāna (meditation).
